nodes.label: Greetings & Set Phrases
nodes.description: Master essential daily greetings, name expressions, and leaving/returning dynamics.
nodes.cheatsheet: Ask names: **Come ti chiami?**. Leaving phrases: **Io vado** (*I'm leaving*) or **Arrivederci**. Home phrasing when coming back: **Sono tornato** (*I'm home*) and **Bentornato** (*Welcome back*).

nodes.label: Countries & Identity
nodes.description: Talk about nationalities, languages, and core family relationships.
nodes.cheatsheet: Nationalities match gender: **Sono italiano** (m) / **italiana** (f). Languages use the country adjective: **Parlo italiano**. For 'Mom', use the affectionate **mamma** or formal **madre**.

nodes.label: Numbers, Hundreds & Thousands
nodes.description: Count higher figures into hundreds and thousands, and learn basic fractions.
nodes.cheatsheet: Hundreds use **cento** (100), **duecento** (200). Thousands: **mille** (1000), **duemila** (2000 - *changes suffix to mila*). Fractions combine cardinal and ordinal components: 2/3 is **due terzi**.

nodes.label: Demonstratives (Questo, Quello, Quale)
nodes.description: Identify objects using proximity-coded demonstrative adjectives.
nodes.cheatsheet: Demonstratives change by gender and vowel initials: **questo/questa** (this). 'That' changes like definite articles: **quello/quella/quel**. 'Which' is **quale**.

nodes.label: Colours & Descriptions
nodes.description: Use basic color vocabulary to modify nouns.
nodes.cheatsheet: Colors must agree with gender/number: **rosso/rossa** (red), **nero** (black), **bianco** (white). Colors ending in '-e' like **verde** (green) or **azzurro** change only for plural (**verdi**).

nodes.label: General Counters
nodes.description: Count distinct objects using gendered plural adaptations.
nodes.cheatsheet: Italian uses direct numerical plurals rather than counter words: **un libro**, **due libri**. Ask 'How many?' using **Quanti?** (m) or **Quante?** (f).

nodes.label: Floors & Negative Numbers
nodes.description: Navigate building levels, floors, basements, and negative figures.
nodes.cheatsheet: Floors use ordinals: **primo piano** (1st floor). Ground floor is **piano terra**. Basement is **seminterrato** or **seminterrato meno uno** (B1). Negative values use **meno** (*meno cinque*).

nodes.label: Buildings & Prepositions
nodes.description: Identify common spatial structures and describe basic physical layouts.
nodes.cheatsheet: Structures: **l'edificio** (building), **la stazione** (station). Positional expressions: **sopra** (on/above), **sotto** (under), **davanti a** (in front of), **dietro** (behind).

nodes.label: Existence & Amount of People
nodes.description: Use existential locatives and count human aggregates.
nodes.cheatsheet: Use the existential locative verbs **c'è** (there is) and **ci sono** (there are). Count people utilizing the noun **persone**: **due persone** (two people).

nodes.label: Relative Time: Day, Week, Month, Year
nodes.description: Anchor timelines cleanly across relative days, weeks, months, and years.
nodes.cheatsheet:
Timeline grid:
Days: **l'altro ieri** → **ieri** → **oggi** → **domani** → **dopodomani**.
Weeks: **la settimana scorsa** → **questa settimana** → **la settimana prossima**.
Months: **il mese scorso** → **questo mese** → **il mese prossimo**.
Years: **l'anno scorso** → **quest'anno** → **l'anno prossimo**.

nodes.label: Specific Time: This Morning & Next Week
nodes.description: Isolate immediate parts of a day or adjacent calendar blocks.
nodes.cheatsheet: Isolate sections: **stamattina** (*this morning*). Next calendar blocks: **la settimana prossima** (*next week*).

nodes.label: Months & Days of the Month
nodes.description: Identify calendar months and navigate normal cardinal calendar dates.
nodes.cheatsheet: Months: **gennaio**, **febbraio**, etc. Dates use regular cardinal numbers preceded by definite articles: 'May 4th' is **il quattro maggio**. Exception for the 1st of the month: **il primo maggio**.

nodes.label: Year & Month Durations
nodes.description: Calculate spans and durations of time using years and month blocks.
nodes.cheatsheet: Calculate spans using **mese/mesi** (month/s) or **anno/anni** (year/s) with the preposition **per**: e.g., *per due mesi* (for two months).

nodes.label: Asking 'When' (Quando)
nodes.description: Formulate open-ended temporal questions about future, present, or past events.
nodes.cheatsheet: Use the question root **Quando?** (*When?*). Example: **Quando vai in Italia?** (*When are you going to Italy?*).

nodes.label: Meals & Food Types
nodes.description: Categorize daily meals and basic dish selections.
nodes.cheatsheet: Meals: **la colazione** (breakfast), **il pranzo** (lunch), **la cena** (dinner). Target dish item: **il sushi**.

nodes.label: Consumptive Actions (Eat & Drink)
nodes.description: Utilize active consumption roots for eating and drinking.
nodes.cheatsheet: Verbs: **mangiare** (to eat) and **bere** (to drink - irregular: *io bevo*). Pair with objects directly: **Mangio il sushi**.

nodes.label: Verb Conjugation & Tenses
nodes.description: Conjugate polite actions into affirmative, negative, past, and exploratory desire states.
nodes.cheatsheet:
Structural expressions using **mangiare**:
Affirmative: **Mangio** (*I eat*)
Negative: **Non mangio il sushi** (*I don't eat sushi*)
Past: Past uses *avere* + participle: **Ho mangiato il sushi** (*I ate sushi*)
Desire: Use modal **volere** → **Voglio mangiare il sushi** (*I want to eat sushi*). Question form: **Vuoi mangiare il sushi?**.

nodes.label: Conjunctions (E anche & Poi)
nodes.description: Link phrases together or order successive events smoothly.
nodes.cheatsheet: Use **E anche / Inoltre** (*And also / Plus*) to add options. Use **Poi / Dopo** (*And then / After that*) to build chronological sequences.

nodes.label: Frequency of Verbs
nodes.description: Quantify how often routines occur using positive and negative adverbs.
nodes.cheatsheet: Scale routines: **spesso** (often), **a volte** (sometimes), **raramente** (rarely), and **non... mai** (never - *requires non before verb*): **Non mangio mai il sushi**.

nodes.label: Transportation & Commuting
nodes.description: Describe modes of travel and ask others how they commute to work.
nodes.cheatsheet: Ask 'How do you go?' via **Come vai al lavoro?**. Vehicles take the preposition **in**: **in aereo** (by plane), **in macchina** (by car), **in nave** (by boat). Walking uses the idiom: **a piedi**.

nodes.label: Sports & Complex Preferences
nodes.description: Express preferences regarding sports, distinguishing what vs what kind.
nodes.cheatsheet: To ask 'What sports...?' use **Quale sport ti piace?**. For 'What kind of sports...?' use **Che genere di sport ti piace?**. Target your choices with the verb **piacere** (e.g., *Mi piace il calcio*).
